name: jmagar-lab
description: Local Codex marketplace for Jacob Magar's Lab homelab plugins.

plugins.name: acp
plugins.description: Agent Client Protocol skills for Rust, Python, and TypeScript implementations.

interface.shortDescription: Agent Client Protocol implementation guidance for Rust, Python, and TypeScript.
interface.longDescription: ACP packages implementation skills and protocol references for building Agent Client Protocol clients and agents, including session lifecycle, streaming, tool calls, and adapter patterns.
defaultPrompt: Use ACP to design a Rust agent-client-protocol integration.
Review this ACP session lifecycle implementation.
Explain how ACP tool calls should stream results.

plugins.name: adguard
plugins.description: Skill for operating adguard via the lab MCP server / CLI.

interface.shortDescription: Operate AdGuard Home DNS filtering and network-blocking workflows.
interface.longDescription: AdGuard Home provides agent workflows for inspecting DNS filtering state, reviewing blocked queries, managing allow/block decisions, and troubleshooting homelab DNS behavior.
defaultPrompt: Use AdGuard to inspect DNS filtering health.
Find recent blocked domains for a client.
Help troubleshoot why a domain is not resolving.

plugins.name: agent-os
plugins.description: Drive the agent-os Windows 11 sandbox VM through its Windows-MCP server. Self-registers the windows-mcp MCP from your configured URL + bearer token, ships the agent-os skill, a /agent-os status command, and a SessionStart health check.

interface.shortDescription: Drive the dedicated Windows 11 agent VM through Windows-MCP.
interface.longDescription: Agent OS connects Codex to the dedicated Windows 11 sandbox VM for desktop automation, Windows app testing, screenshots, shell commands, and VM health checks.
defaultPrompt: Use Agent OS to inspect the Windows VM.
Run a PowerShell command on agent-os.
Take a screenshot of the agent-os desktop.

plugins.name: arrs
plugins.description: The *arr / media-automation stack in one plugin: Radarr, Sonarr, Prowlarr, Overseerr, SABnzbd, qBittorrent, Plex, Jellyfin, Tautulli, and Tracearr — each operated via its own REST API. Credentials are configured here and bridged to the skills via a generated env file.

interface.shortDescription: Operate the Radarr, Sonarr, Prowlarr, Plex, Jellyfin, and downloader stack.
interface.longDescription: Arrs bundles media-automation skills for Radarr, Sonarr, Prowlarr, Overseerr, SABnzbd, qBittorrent, Plex, Jellyfin, Tautulli, and Tracearr using each service API directly.
defaultPrompt: Use Arrs to check the media stack.
Find stalled downloads across the Arr stack.
Search for a movie and prepare a Radarr add request.

plugins.name: beads
plugins.description: AI-supervised issue tracker for coding workflows.

interface.shortDescription: AI-supervised issue tracker with beads for task creation, progress tracking, and dependency management.
interface.longDescription: Beads is a lightweight issue tracker purpose-built for AI coding workflows. Create issues before writing code, claim work, track dependencies, close on completion, and search persistent knowledge — all via the bd CLI. Replaces TodoWrite and markdown task files with a durable, cross-session store.
defaultPrompt: Use Beads to list ready work.
Create a Beads issue for this task.
Show blocked Beads issues and their dependencies.

plugins.name: bitwarden
plugins.description: Bitwarden and secrets-management workflows for homelab operations.

interface.shortDescription: Work with Bitwarden vault items and secrets-management flows.
interface.longDescription: Bitwarden provides secure agent workflows for finding vault items, handling unlocked CLI sessions, and supporting homelab credential lookup without hardcoding secrets.
defaultPrompt: Use Bitwarden to find the credential for this service.
Check whether the Bitwarden CLI session is unlocked.
Help prepare a secret lookup without exposing the value.

plugins.name: broadcastr
plugins.description: Real-time activity broadcast across concurrent Claude Code sessions. Captures commits and plan/session document activity into a shared JSONL bus; each session sees what others are doing.

interface.shortDescription: Broadcast session activity across concurrent agent work.
interface.longDescription: Broadcastr writes commits, plans, and session activity to a shared JSONL bus so concurrent coding agents can see what related sessions are doing in real time.
defaultPrompt: Use Broadcastr to summarize recent session activity.
Show what other agents have changed recently.
Check the activity bus before I edit this repo.

plugins.name: bytestash
plugins.description: Skills for operating a ByteStash snippet manager.

interface.shortDescription: Manage ByteStash snippets and reusable code notes.
interface.longDescription: ByteStash provides snippet search, creation, update, organization, and troubleshooting workflows for the self-hosted code snippet manager.
defaultPrompt: Use ByteStash to search for a Docker snippet.
Create a ByteStash snippet from this script.
Organize snippets related to Rust.

plugins.name: dozzle
plugins.description: Skill for operating Dozzle through direct HTTP API checks, auth guidance, and MCP setup notes.

interface.shortDescription: Inspect Docker container logs through Dozzle and its native MCP endpoint.
interface.longDescription: Dozzle provides direct HTTP and MCP-oriented workflows for checking live Docker container logs, authentication setup, and log-viewer connectivity.
defaultPrompt: Use Dozzle to inspect recent container logs.
Check whether Dozzle MCP is reachable.
Help debug a container error from Dozzle logs.

plugins.name: immich
plugins.description: Skill for operating immich via the lab MCP server / CLI.

interface.shortDescription: Operate Immich photo and video management workflows.
interface.longDescription: Immich packages workflows for searching assets, reviewing albums and jobs, checking server state, and assisting with self-hosted photo library operations.
defaultPrompt: Use Immich to inspect recent upload status.
Search Immich for assets from a date range.
Check Immich server health.

plugins.name: linkding
plugins.description: Skills for operating a Linkding bookmark manager.

interface.shortDescription: Search and manage Linkding bookmarks.
interface.longDescription: Linkding provides bookmark search, creation, update, tagging, and cleanup workflows for the self-hosted bookmark manager.
defaultPrompt: Use Linkding to find bookmarks about Rust MCP.
Save this URL to Linkding with tags.
Summarize my recent Linkding bookmarks.

plugins.name: loggifly
plugins.description: Skill for operating loggifly via the lab MCP server / CLI.

interface.shortDescription: Configure Docker log alerting with keyword and regex rules.
interface.longDescription: LoggiFly helps inspect and manage Docker container log alerting rules, keyword patterns, regex checks, and notification routing for homelab services.
defaultPrompt: Use LoggiFly to inspect active alert rules.
Add a log alert pattern for this container.
Check recent LoggiFly alert matches.

plugins.name: lavra
plugins.description: 30 specialized agents, 28 commands, 15 skills, persistent memory with auto-recall.

interface.shortDescription: 30 specialized agents, 28 commands, 15 skills, and persistent memory with auto-recall.
interface.longDescription: Lavra is a comprehensive agent and skills toolkit covering brainstorming, planning, research, feature development, code review, QA, design, and retrospectives. It ships 30 specialized agents, 28 slash commands, 15 skills, and a persistent cross-session memory system.
defaultPrompt: Use Lavra to plan and implement this feature.
Run a Lavra code review on recent changes.
Use Lavra brainstorming to explore this design.

plugins.name: memos
plugins.description: Skills for operating a Memos note hub.

interface.shortDescription: Manage notes and memos in the self-hosted Memos service.
interface.longDescription: Memos provides workflows for searching, creating, updating, and organizing short notes in a self-hosted Memos instance.
defaultPrompt: Use Memos to search for notes about this project.
Create a memo from this summary.
List recent Memos entries.

plugins.name: navidrome
plugins.description: Operate a self-hosted Navidrome music server through its Subsonic API with direct HTTP calls — ping, browse artists/albums, search, and list playlists.

interface.shortDescription: Browse and inspect a Navidrome music library through the Subsonic API.
interface.longDescription: Navidrome provides read-only music-library workflows for pinging the server, browsing artists and albums, searching tracks, listing playlists, and checking now-playing state.
defaultPrompt: Use Navidrome to search my music library.
List Navidrome playlists.
Check whether Navidrome is reachable.

plugins.name: neo4j
plugins.description: Skill for operating neo4j via the lab MCP server / CLI.

interface.shortDescription: Run Neo4j graph queries and inspect graph schema.
interface.longDescription: Neo4j bundles Cypher query, schema inspection, node and relationship exploration, and graph-database troubleshooting workflows.
defaultPrompt: Use Neo4j to inspect the graph schema.
Run a Cypher query for related nodes.
Explain this Neo4j query plan.

plugins.name: notebooklm
plugins.description: NotebookLM research, source, generation, and download workflows.

interface.shortDescription: Operate NotebookLM sources, notebooks, and generated research artifacts.
interface.longDescription: NotebookLM provides programmatic workflows for managing sources, querying notebooks, generating summaries, and downloading research outputs.
defaultPrompt: Use NotebookLM to summarize this notebook.
Add this source to NotebookLM.
List recent NotebookLM generated outputs.

plugins.name: plexus
plugins.description: Remote-device memory and live operating context for host-specific work.

interface.shortDescription: Host-specific REMOTE.md memory with live infrastructure probes.
interface.longDescription: Plexus gives agents a CLAUDE.md-style memory file for each remote host, then combines it with live SSH, Tailscale, Docker, systemd, and log context before operating.
defaultPrompt: Use Plexus to inspect squirts before changing it.
Load live remote context for dookie.
Summarize recent host activity.

plugins.name: qdrant
plugins.description: Skill for operating qdrant via the lab MCP server / CLI.

interface.shortDescription: Inspect and manage Qdrant vector collections.
interface.longDescription: Qdrant provides workflows for collections, points, payloads, search, vector database health, and semantic-search troubleshooting.
defaultPrompt: Use Qdrant to list collections.
Inspect this Qdrant collection configuration.
Search Qdrant for similar vectors.

plugins.name: radicale
plugins.description: CalDAV and CardDAV workflow skills for Radicale.

interface.shortDescription: Manage CalDAV calendars and CardDAV contacts through Radicale.
interface.longDescription: Radicale packages calendar and contact workflows for inspecting collections, managing events or contacts, and troubleshooting CalDAV/CardDAV state.
defaultPrompt: Use Radicale to inspect calendar collections.
Find contacts matching this query.
Check whether the CalDAV endpoint is reachable.

plugins.name: scrutiny
plugins.description: Skill for operating scrutiny via the lab MCP server / CLI.

interface.shortDescription: Inspect SMART drive health through Scrutiny.
interface.longDescription: Scrutiny provides workflows for checking disk SMART status, drive health, temperature, failure indicators, and homelab storage monitoring data.
defaultPrompt: Use Scrutiny to check drive health.
Show drives with SMART warnings.
Summarize recent disk-health changes.

plugins.name: swag-mcp
plugins.description: SWAG reverse proxy configuration management via MCP. Create, edit, view, and manage nginx proxy configurations with auth integration.

interface.shortDescription: Manage SWAG reverse-proxy configuration through MCP workflows.
interface.longDescription: SWAG MCP helps create, edit, view, back up, and validate SWAG nginx reverse-proxy configs with auth integration and local or SSH-backed storage.
defaultPrompt: Use SWAG MCP to create a proxy config.
Validate this nginx proxy configuration.
Inspect existing SWAG configs for a service.

plugins.name: superpowers
plugins.description: Core skills library: TDD, debugging, collaboration patterns, and proven techniques.

interface.shortDescription: Core skills library for TDD, systematic debugging, collaboration patterns, and engineering discipline.
interface.longDescription: Superpowers provides foundational coding discipline skills: test-driven development, systematic debugging, code review workflows, git worktrees, plan writing, subagent-driven development, and verification before completion. Each skill enforces a proven technique to reduce errors and improve output quality.
defaultPrompt: Use Superpowers TDD to implement this feature.
Use Superpowers systematic-debugging to diagnose this failure.
Use Superpowers to review and verify this change before merging.

plugins.name: tei
plugins.description: Skill for operating tei via the lab MCP server / CLI.

interface.shortDescription: Use Hugging Face Text Embeddings Inference for embeddings and reranking.
interface.longDescription: TEI provides workflows for checking Text Embeddings Inference health, embedding text, tokenizing input, and reranking documents against a query.
defaultPrompt: Use TEI to embed this text.
Check TEI health and model info.
Rerank these documents for this query.

plugins.name: testing
plugins.description: App-testing and MCP-tooling skills: live QA of web, Android, and desktop apps; MCP server smoke-testing (mcporter); MCP-UI / Apps validation (mcpjam); and claude-in-mobile device automation.

interface.shortDescription: Run web, Android, desktop, MCP, and app-validation testing workflows.
interface.longDescription: Testing bundles end-to-end QA skills for browser apps, Android APKs, Windows desktop apps, MCP smoke tests, MCP-UI validation, and mobile device automation.
defaultPrompt: Use Testing to run a web app QA pass.
Smoke-test this MCP server.
Test this Android APK end to end.

plugins.name: uptime-kuma
plugins.description: Read-only monitoring of a self-hosted Uptime Kuma instance via direct HTTP — Prometheus /metrics (API-key auth) and public status-page JSON. No monitor management (that requires Uptime Kuma's socket.io API).

interface.shortDescription: Read Uptime Kuma monitor, metrics, and public status-page state.
interface.longDescription: Uptime Kuma provides read-only monitoring workflows for Prometheus metrics, API-key checks, public status-page JSON, heartbeat history, and uptime summaries.
defaultPrompt: Use Uptime Kuma to check monitor status.
Summarize the public status page.
Find monitors with recent downtime.

plugins.name: vibin
plugins.description: Session, GitHub review, and CI workflow skills for quick push, session documentation, PR comments, and CI debugging.

interface.shortDescription: Session, GitHub review, CI, and agent workflow utilities.
interface.longDescription: Vibin bundles session documentation, quick-push, GitHub PR review, CI debugging, Windows VM, screenshot, clipboard, and homelab workflow skills.
defaultPrompt: Use Vibin to quick-push this change.
Use Vibin to address PR review comments.
Save this session to Markdown.
